instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Can You Buy Multiple Term Insurance Policies?
To achieve your long-term financial goals and provide monetary security to your loved ones, investing in a term insurance plan may be crucial. It may safeguard you and your family against unforeseen risks associated with your life. In an unfortunate scenario where you lose your life within the policy’s tenure, your nominee(s) may receive the sum assured as a death benefit. It may act as an income alternative that your family can count on.
But will coverage under one policy be enough? And more so, can more than one term policy be bought by the same individual? While the answer to the second question is an affirmative one, it may be important to know more about buying multiple plans before doing so.
Why People May opt for Multiple Term Insurance Plans [1]
One may assume that purchasing multiple term insurance plans to achieve higher coverage may seem turn out to be expensive than purchasing just one. But there may be some benefits involved that may justify the purchase of multiple plans. For example, you may want to diversify your term insurance coverage among various insurers if the coverage amount is substantial. You may want to do this to deal with large and small claims, both of which may have varying settlement durations. You may get some of the smaller claims settled with an insurer, which a larger claim takes it’s time to get settled with another insurer. Additionally, each insurance company may come with its own underwriting policies, which may impact the maximum permitted sum assured amount. For example, if an individual's health condition prevents them from qualifying for a term plan of Rs 1 crore as per their underwriting policy, they may opt for multiple term insurance plans with different sum assured amounts that may total up to Rs 1 crore.
What are the Advantages of Having Multiple Term Insurance Plans?
There are several benefits you may get from having more than one term insurance plan -
Higher probability of claim approvals
By purchasing multiple term insurance policies, you increase the likelihood of having your claim approved. When selecting an insurance policy, it's crucial to assess the claim settlement ratio, which refers to the proportion of claims processed by the insurer versus the number of claims filed in a given year. If the settlement ratio is high, it means there's a greater chance of your claim being accepted, even if it's been declined by another insurer. [2]
Surplus coverage
Enjoying extensive coverage can be a key benefit of buying more than two life insurance plans. If you have purchased a term plan before but have realised that your coverage needs may have increased over the years and will continue to do so, you may think of going for a second term insurance plan to ensure your life cover needs are met. With multiple life insurance plans, you can also select different family members/people as nominees.
Tax benefits
You may claim tax benefits on the premiums you pay for all the term insurance plans you own. The condition is that you can claim a maximum deduction of up to Rs 1,50,000 every year as per Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961[3]. Also, the death benefits that your nominee receives are exempt from taxes according to Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961 [4]
What is the Claim Process for Multiple Term Insurance Policies?
While buying an insurance policy, you might have to provide comprehensive information to your insurance provider about all your existing life insurance plans. For the claims, there is usually the same claim process that your nominee may have to follow for all other life insurance plans that you own[6]. The process being-
The nominee need to submit the duly filled claim form along with all the required documents including policy papers and the death certificate of the policyholder to the insurer
Once the required submission is done, the insurance provider will evaluate the claim and pay the benefit under the plan, subject to satisfaction of the terms and conditions of the said policy.
You can contact your insurance provider to find further details regarding the claim process of your term plan.
How can you buy multiple term insurance plans?
If you're planning to purchase multiple insurance plans, the below pointers may help you in streamlining the process and save time as well-
Assessing your coverage needs from time-to-time
It may help to regularly assess your insurance needs to determine if you require additional/multiple insurance plans. You may consider changes in income or any new familial responsibilities to ensure purchasing additional insurance policies may not create a financial strain.
Exploring different term plans online
You may explore different insurance plans you find suitable online, and then compare them to shortlist and choose. Looking at reviews from experts on various life insurance plans offered by different companies may help you narrow your choices. And while you’re online, you may also use a term insurance calculator to estimate your coverage needs.
Keep all insurance providers in the loop [7]
When you buy multiple term plans from different life insurance providers, you may ensure each of them is aware of the other policies. You may provide minimum basic details of the term policies you own, along with their maturity date. Keeping everyone informed may help you avoid any issues at the time of claim. Knowing about your previous life insurance policies may also allow insurers to understand your coverage needs better.
Term insurance may be a cost-effective method of ensuring financial security for your loved ones in the event of your absence. Therefore, investing in multiple term insurance policies may provide various benefits. When purchasing multiple policies, make sure to compare the coverage and terms and conditions offered by different insurers to make an informed decision.
References -
1. https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/pick-of-the-day/pros-and-cons-of-having-multiple-term-insurance-plans/article37985755.ece
2. https://www.forbes.com/advisor/in/life-insurance/what-you-need-to-know-about-claims-paid-ratio-in-a-life-insurance-policy/
3. https://news.cleartax.in/why-you-must-buy-a-term-insurance-plan-how-is-it-beneficial/5080/
4. https://cleartax.in/s/section-10-of-income-tax-act
5. https://www.moneycontrol.com/news/business/personal-finance/budget-2023-tax-exemption-for-traditional-insurance-policies-9978941.html
6. https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/term/term-insurance-claim-process.html
7. https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/term/multiple-term-insurance-plans.html
Disclaimer -
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789
Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility.
$ Subject to the passing of the Finance bill in the Parliament
